extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won the bunch sprint in Kaposvár after launching with 300m to go, perhaps early but holding power through the line.
tactical_patterns: EENKHOORN Pascal attacked late for KOM points despite being leadout rider, revealing split priorities within team roles.
Quick-Step positioned CAVENDISH Mark at front with 300m remaining, unconventionally early for modern sprints but negated positioning chaos.
ALPECIN-FENIX controlled with VAN DER POEL Mathieu in leadout mode final 1km, signaling sprint support hierarchy shift.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: launched 300m out, conventionally early timing, but sustained power through line suggests superior condition over pure positioning sprinters
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: finished second despite losing CAVENDISH wheel, indicates strong independent sprint without perfect leadout
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: podium finish and won intermediate sprint suggests form returning after multi-season decline
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: neutral: top-five finish without optimal positioning reveals raw speed but immature sprint craft
rider_signals.EENKHOORN Pascal: neutral: attacked solo for KOM points 6km from sprint finish, conflicting team duties with NIZZOLO Giacomo leadout responsibilities
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: deployed as leadout rider rather than sprint finisher, confirms no sprint ambitions in flat stages
